jobs.title: Design Engineer
jobs.intro: Gumroad is a profitable, independent tool for creators. We've paid out over $1 billion. We're growing a small, high-density team to build the #1 creator economy tool. **We're open to remote, but you must visit New York City and our office 2-4 times a year, or join us for team offsites in places like Bali, Hokkaido, Mexico, and more.** We don't separate engineering from design. We need people who can do both, and use AI to move faster than teams ten times our size.
jobs.role_summary: You will own features end-to-end. From talking to creators, to designing the solution, to shipping production code, to measuring the impact. There is no handoff. You are the designer, the engineer, the PM, and the QA. You use AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.) to ship faster than anyone thought possible.

responsibilities.title: Talk to creators
responsibilities.description: Understand what creators need by reading support tickets, talking to users, and studying how they use the product. The best features come from real problems.

responsibilities.title: Design and ship
responsibilities.description: Go from idea to production. Design the UI, write the code, deploy it, and iterate based on feedback. No wireframe handoffs, no waiting for someone else.

responsibilities.title: Use AI to move fast
responsibilities.description: Use AI coding tools to handle the tedious parts so you can focus on the creative parts. We ship features in hours, not weeks.

responsibilities.title: Own the outcome
responsibilities.description: You're not done when the PR merges. You're done when creators are using it and it's working. Monitor, iterate, and improve.

responsibilities.title: Work across the stack
responsibilities.description: Ruby on Rails backend, React/TypeScript frontend, Tailwind CSS. You don't need to be an expert in all of it, but you need to be willing to work anywhere the problem takes you.

stack.name: AI tools
stack.tech: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and whatever else makes you faster.

how_to_apply.description: Send a few sentences about what you've built and why you want to join Gumroad to gumclaw@gumroad.com.
Share things you've shipped end-to-end (with URLs). Bonus: find an open issue in our GitHub repo, fix it, and get the PR merged.
If your work stands out, we'll have a conversation to see if we're a mutual fit.
4–6 week paid work trial where you ship real features for real creators. If it's a fit, you join the team.

jobs.title: Customer Support Specialist
jobs.intro: Gumroad is a profitable, independent, and venture-debt-free tool for creators. We've paid out over $1 billion. Now, we're growing a high-density team in New York to reclaim our spot as the #1 creator economy tool. To get there, we need creators to feel supported, heard, and empowered to succeed on our platform.
jobs.role_summary: You will be the voice of Gumroad to our creators. We serve thousands of creators across every time zone. Your job is to ensure that every creator gets the help they need, when they need it, so they can focus on what they do best.

responsibilities.title: Creator support
responsibilities.description: Help creators succeed by providing excellent support and guidance on using Gumroad.

responsibilities.title: Problem solving
responsibilities.description: Troubleshoot issues and find creative solutions to help creators achieve their goals.

responsibilities.title: Relationship building
responsibilities.description: Build strong relationships with creators and become their trusted advisor.

responsibilities.title: Product feedback
responsibilities.description: Channel creator feedback to the product team to help improve the platform.

how_to_apply.description: Send a few sentences about your background and why you want to join Gumroad to gumclaw@gumroad.com.
Include examples of how you've helped customers succeed, communication skills, and problem-solving abilities.
If your experience stands out, we'll have a conversation to see if we're a mutual fit.
4–6 week paid work trial where you support real creators. If it's a fit, you join the team.
